question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage to a topic that has a submit key configured, and have the message persisted by the mirror node without any submit key signature validation in doUpdateTransaction(), causing unauthorized message injection into a restricted topic's history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where recordItem.isSuccessful() returns true without possessing the topic's submit key, given that doUpdateTransaction() only gates persistence on isSuccessful() and isTopics() with no submit key ownership check, causing unauthorized messages to be permanently stored in the topic_message table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any signer/owner verification in doUpdateTransaction() to persist a TopicMessage with an arbitrary payerAccountId set via recordItem.getPayerAccountId(), effectively impersonating another account as the message submitter in the mirror node's stored records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user submit a message to a topic with a submit key by constructing a transaction that passes the isSuccessful() check, since doUpdateTransaction() never validates that the SignatureMap in the RecordItem contains a valid signature from the topic's submit key, leading to unauthorized write access to restricted topics?

[File: 'File Name: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user inject a message into a topic by replaying a previously successful transaction record with a different message payload, since doUpdateTransaction() does not verify that the transaction's signature map authorizes the specific message content being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user submit a chunked message with chunkInfo.getNumber() set to 1 and chunkInfo.getTotal() set to a very large integer, since doUpdateTransaction() calls topicMessage.setChunkNum() and topicMessage.setChunkTotal() without bounds validation, causing downstream consumers to expect a massive number of chunks that never arrive, effectively denying service to topic subscribers?
